steps.title: Relate the cone lengths
steps.content:
The height, radius, and segment $AB$ form a right triangle with hypotenuse $32$ and one leg $16$. Use the Pythagorean relationship to find the radius squared.
$$r^2+16^2=32^2$$
$$r^2=32^2-16^2$$
$$r^2=1{,}024-256$$
$$r^2=768$$

steps.title: Use the cone volume formula
steps.content:
The volume of a cone is one-third the product of the base area and the height. Substitute $r^2=768$ and $h=16$.
$$V=(1/3)\pi(768)(16)=4{,}096\pi$$

steps.title: Match the coefficient
steps.content: The volume is given as $k\pi$, so compare that form with $4{,}096\pi$. This gives $k=4{,}096$. The answer is 4096.
